title: External Integrations, Distributed Effects, and Resilience · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for external integrations, distributed effects, and resilience and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for controlled integration and resilience evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.
Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.
Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Integration Authority · changed case · Quiz Integration Authority
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

Which action demonstrates Integration Authority without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Integration Authority · changed case · Quiz Integration Authority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries. For Integration Authority · changed case · Quiz Integration Authority, keep Integration Authority connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Integration Authority result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NACE Career Readiness Competencies — Controls reflection and evidence for career and self-development, communication, critical thinking, leadership, professionalism, teamwork, technology, and inclusive practice.

The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

For Integration Authority · changed case · Quiz Integration Authority, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An API key and SDK establish permission to use a service.

stimulus.title: Integration Authority case evidence
stimulus.caption: Inspect the observation, governing requirement, and mastery evidence before deciding.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: An API key and SDK establish permission to use a service.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.

options.text: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for controlled integration and resilience evidence.
An API key and SDK establish permission to use a service. That claim fails the integration authority · changed case evidence boundary for Integration Authority.

For integration authority · changed case, apply Integration Authority without its stated preconditions, omit the controlled integration and resilience evidence trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Integration Authority for controlled integration and resilience evidence.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Vendor Exit
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Vendor Exit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Vendor Exit, keep Vendor Exit connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.
